name: SF2B Author continuity lock rejects route milestone regression

source.purpose: Ensures SF2B Ch2 Author validation rejects an opening that makes an already-taken route fork upcoming again.

thread_tail_cutter.title: The unresolved tail and cutter
thread_tail_cutter.resolutionCriteria: Identify whether the cutter shadowing Redline/Carrow belongs to Calla, a rival, or the cargo's owner.
thread_tail_cutter.failureMode: The cutter forces the route before the cargo terms are understood.
thread_tail_cutter.retrievalCue: tail, cutter silhouette, sealed Redline/Carrow cargo

thread_redline_carrow_cargo.title: The sealed Redline/Carrow cargo
thread_redline_carrow_cargo.resolutionCriteria: Determine what obligation the sealed cargo creates before committing to Redline.
thread_redline_carrow_cargo.failureMode: The cargo becomes leverage before the PC knows who owns the risk.
thread_redline_carrow_cargo.retrievalCue: sealed cargo, Redline/Carrow route, Calla's brokerage

npc_calla_voss.name: Calla Voss

voice.note: smiles only after the price lands

npc_calla_voss.retrievalCue: Calla Voss, broker, sealed Redline/Carrow cargo

location_driftrunner_transit.name: Driftrunner - Transit Bridge
location_driftrunner_transit.description: The Driftrunner's bridge after the Redline fork.

promise_calla_delivery.obligation: Do not open or reroute the sealed cargo before Calla names the buyer.

meaning.situation: The Driftrunner has already taken the Redline fork with Calla Voss's sealed cargo aboard.
meaning.tension: The cutter still follows after the fork, turning the Carrow cover leg into old cover rather than a future route choice.
meaning.ticking: The passive display shows the cutter holding four ships back after the fork crossing.
meaning.question: Who is following the Driftrunner after the Redline fork?
meaning.closer: The fork is behind the ship, but the cutter is not.

sceneSummaries.summary: The Driftrunner took the Redline fork, left the Carrow cover leg behind, and kept the sealed cargo cold while the cutter followed.

currentLocation.name: Driftrunner - Transit Bridge
currentLocation.description: The Driftrunner's bridge after the Redline fork.

location.name: Driftrunner - Transit Bridge
location.description: The Driftrunner's bridge after the Redline fork.

established: The Driftrunner has already taken the Redline fork.
The Carrow cover leg is behind the ship.
A cutter still follows four ships back.
Cryo container holding temperature, sealed in hold.

turns.playerInput: I take the Redline fork and keep the passive sweep on the cutter.
turns.narratorProse: The Driftrunner takes the Redline fork. Carrow falls behind as cover, not destination, while the sealed cryo container holds temperature in the dark.

turns.playerInput: I watch whether the cutter follows after the fork.
turns.narratorProse: Four ships back, the cutter follows after the fork crossing, close enough to be intent and far enough to pretend otherwise.

chapter_frame.title: The Chit Burns
chapter_frame.premise: The Vethara hail turns the Driftrunner's sealed Redline cargo into a legal problem.
chapter_frame.active_pressure: A corporate warrant tests whether the PC will negotiate before the destination decrypts.
chapter_frame.central_tension: Answer Sollar now or learn the destination first.
chapter_frame.chapter_scope: The Driftrunner bridge under a live corporate hail.
chapter_frame.objective: Decide how to handle Sollar while the chit decrypts.
chapter_frame.crucible: The PC must choose between information control and legal exposure.

outcome_spectrum.clean: Kessrin still calls you back; Laine promise still feels reachable.
outcome_spectrum.costly: Renn now treats you as an asset; Patel debt is owed.
outcome_spectrum.failure: Vasek now has your transponder ID; Sable contact is burned.
outcome_spectrum.catastrophic: Solitaire knows your face; the corridor hunts your registry forever.

opening_scene_spec.initial_state: The Driftrunner runs dark with Calla Voss's sealed cryo container stable, but the Redline fork is coming up in the nav buffer.
opening_scene_spec.first_player_facing: Sollar's Vethara hail arrives while the cutter holds four ships back after the Carrow cover leg.
opening_scene_spec.immediate_choice: Answer Sollar now or wait for the chit destination before speaking.
opening_scene_spec.dramatic_situation: Sollar times legal contact to Calla's cargo secret so silence and speech both become evidence.
opening_scene_spec.first_visible_pressure: The Vethara hail arrives before the PC can control what the chit reveals.
opening_scene_spec.first_human_or_institutional_move: Sollar uses the warrant hail to make the PC's next answer legally useful.
withheld_premise_facts: The cutter may be Vethara's collection shadow.

antagonist_field.core_pressure: Legal contact can become route control before the cargo destination is known.

possible_faces.name: Renn Sollar
possible_faces.becomes_primary_when: The PC lets the hail frame the cargo.

antagonist_field.escalation_logic: Every silence lets Vethara make the Driftrunner's route look evasive.

starting_npcs.name: Calla Voss
starting_npcs.hidden_pressure: Her second-layer chit controls the destination.
starting_npcs.disposition_reason: her cargo is aboard and still opaque

starting_npcs.name: Renn Sollar
starting_npcs.hidden_pressure: Needs voluntary contact to widen authority.
starting_npcs.disposition_reason: he treats silent cargo runners as warrant targets

starting_npcs.name: Lyra Keel
starting_npcs.voice_note: talks like she is already leaving
starting_npcs.hidden_pressure: Can confirm whether the cutter mirrored the fork.
starting_npcs.disposition_reason: she has no stake beyond avoiding Vethara heat

active_threads.title: The unresolved tail
active_threads.question: Who owns the cutter?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: Identify whether the cutter belongs to Calla, Vethara, a rival, or the cargo's owner.
active_threads.failure_mode: The cutter forces the Driftrunner before Sollar's leverage is understood.
active_threads.retrieval_cue: cutter silhouette, four ships back, Redline fork

active_threads.title: The sealed cargo
active_threads.question: What does the cargo require?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: Learn the cargo destination and terms without breaking Calla's seal.
active_threads.failure_mode: The cargo becomes legal leverage before the destination is known.
active_threads.retrieval_cue: sealed cryo container, Calla chit, Redline/Carrow

active_threads.title: Sollar's warrant hail
active_threads.question: Will the PC answer?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: The PC answers, refuses, or reframes Sollar's legal contact.
active_threads.failure_mode: Sollar turns nonresponse into authority over the Driftrunner.
active_threads.retrieval_cue: Sollar hail, Vethara tag, warrant pressure

active_threads.title: The destination chit
active_threads.question: What will decrypt?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: The destination decrypts and becomes actionable.
active_threads.failure_mode: The PC negotiates blind while Vethara records intent.

arc_link.chapter_function: Turn cargo uncertainty into corporate contact pressure.
arc_link.player_stance_read: The PC favors information control before compliance.

pacing_contract.chapter_question: Can the PC control Sollar before the cargo terms become unavoidable?
pacing_contract.middle_pressure: The chit gives a destination with strings.

continuation_moves.prior_chapter_meaning: The Driftrunner left Sable Notch with sealed cargo and an unresolved cutter tail.
continuation_moves.larger_pattern_revealed: Vethara treats blind cargo routes as legal handles.

new_named_threat_from_prior_success.name: Renn Sollar
new_named_threat_from_prior_success.why_inevitable: Vethara watches sealed traffic after Redline.

worsened_existing_thread.prior_small_detail: the cutter stayed four ships back
worsened_existing_thread.why_load_bearing_now: its silence lets Sollar speak first

planted_midchapter_revelation.hidden_statement: The cutter is not Vethara but benefits from the hail.
planted_midchapter_revelation.recontextualizes: Sollar is pressure, not the whole pursuit.

continuation_dramatic_turn.prior_chapter_meant: The PC chose Calla's sealed Redline cargo and proved willing to run the deception layer.
continuation_dramatic_turn.larger_pattern_revealed: Vethara turns blind cargo timing into voluntary legal exposure.

pressure_owner.why_they_now_act: He can exploit the chit unlock window before the PC controls the destination facts.

human_leverage.what_they_can_take_or_offer: Sollar can offer procedural cleanliness or mark the Driftrunner as evasive.
human_leverage.what_they_need_from_pc: He needs the PC to speak before the cargo destination becomes defensible.

worsened_detail.prior_detail: Calla controlled the second-layer chit timing.
worsened_detail.why_it_is_load_bearing_now: The hail proves someone else knew the timing window.

continuation_dramatic_turn.offscreen_antagonist_presence: The cutter stays four ships back as proof of reach, not as a direct boarding scene.

procedure_budget.dramatic_function: The timing makes either silence or answer a legal posture.

pressure_ladder.pressure: Sollar opens a contact record that gives Vethara leverage.
pressure_ladder.trigger_condition: Sollar names the Driftrunner and asks for confirmation.
pressure_ladder.narrative_effect: The PC risks making silence or speech evidence.

pressure_ladder.pressure: The chit names a destination under warrant heat.
pressure_ladder.trigger_condition: The destination decrypts while Sollar remains active.
pressure_ladder.narrative_effect: The PC must decide what to reveal.

pressure_ladder.pressure: The cutter makes its alignment visible.
pressure_ladder.trigger_condition: The PC commits to a stance against Sollar.
pressure_ladder.narrative_effect: The shadow pressure stops being ambiguous.

tension_score.pressure: The cutter's owner remains unknown after the fork.
tension_score.prose_surface: Four ships back becomes a measurement of intent.
tension_score.advances_when: The PC exposes route intent or tests the tail.
tension_score.resolves_or_reframes_when: The cutter declares, breaks, or is identified.

tension_score.pressure: The sealed cargo's destination is still controlled by Calla's chit.
tension_score.prose_surface: The reader makes ignorance visible by percentage.
tension_score.advances_when: The chit decrypts or the PC opens the cargo.

tension_score.pressure: Sollar makes contact feel like compliance.
tension_score.prose_surface: His patience turns every delay into posture.
tension_score.advances_when: The PC speaks, stalls, or refuses the hail.
tension_score.resolves_or_reframes_when: Sollar's authority is accepted, narrowed, or broken.

possible_revelations.statement: The cutter is not Vethara's asset.
possible_revelations.emergence_condition: Lyra compares the cutter's lane behavior to Vethara procedure.
possible_revelations.recontextualizes: The hail and tail are overlapping pressures.

possible_revelations.statement: The destination chit can incriminate voluntary intent.
possible_revelations.emergence_condition: Sollar gets the PC to name destination knowledge.
possible_revelations.recontextualizes: Reading first is useful but not free.

escalation_options.condition: The PC answers without naming cargo.
escalation_options.consequence: Sollar narrows questions to destination knowledge.
The cutter's spacing becomes easier to read.
The destination becomes hard to deny.

editorialized_lore.relevance_now: Sollar wants the PC to speak first.
editorialized_lore.delivery_method: He asks for confirmation, not compliance.

editorialized_lore.item: Blind chits split route from intent.
editorialized_lore.relevance_now: Reading the chit changes legal posture.
editorialized_lore.delivery_method: The reader percentage interrupts the hail.

human_stakes.what_is_lost: Their trust stops protecting the PC unless repaired.
The PC becomes known as too costly to help.
